title: The pattern I am most afraid of repeating
subtitle: A journalling set for naming the family money behaviour you most fear finding in yourself.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for bringing the feared family pattern into the open: naming it, understanding what drove it, separating your circumstances from theirs, and deciding what you choose instead. Five prompts, twenty to thirty minutes.
sourceNotes: Drawn from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 2 prompts on breaking generational money patterns.

practice.whenToUse: Use when fear of repeating a specific family money behaviour is active and you want to examine it directly.

content.text: A journal set for looking directly at the family money behaviour you most dread finding in yourself. Five prompts. Allow twenty to thirty minutes.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What does fear of repeating the family pattern feel like right now?
In your chest, your shoulders, your spending choices. Write what's there.
What is the pattern you are most afraid of repeating?
Name it plainly. Overspending, avoiding bills, fighting about money, never having enough.
What was driving the person you watched doing this?
What were they afraid of? What did they not have access to that you do?
Have you already repeated the pattern, or are you afraid you might?
If you have, write what happened. If you're afraid, write what the scenario looks like.
What one decision, made today, would start the pattern moving in a different direction?
Name the smallest version of that decision that is available right now.
Drawn from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 2.
